name: Columbia (United States)

colors.symbolism: the principal field; deep blue of the Carolina sky and the city's rivers
intermediate diagonal band suggesting the convergence of rivers
innermost diagonal band evoking the daytime sky over the Midlands
the eight-pointed star marking the city's place as the state capital

description: A navy field crossed by two diagonal bands — one medium blue, one light blue — descending from the upper fly to the lower hoist in nested fashion, with a white eight-pointed star in the upper-hoist corner.
history: Columbia, South Carolina's municipal flag uses graduated diagonal blue bands to represent the rivers and skies of the Midlands and a star to mark the city as state capital.

features.symbolism: Three nested diagonal bands of blue — dark navy, medium blue and pale blue — descend from the upper fly to the lower hoist, evoking the rivers and skies of the Midlands of South Carolina.

features.role: compass-rose star at the upper hoist
features.symbolism: Marks Columbia as the capital of South Carolina; the eight points evoke a compass rose at the centre of the state.
